source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Cluster Auditing is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Cluster Auditing is Enabled
input_row.description: Send	cluster	audit	logs	to	a	remote	aggregator	to	mitigate	the	risk	of	local	tampering	in	the event	of	a	breach.
input_row.rationale: Exporting	cluster	audit	logs	to	a	dedicated,	persistent	datastore	such	as	Log	Service	(SLS) ensures	availability	of	audit	data	following	a	cluster	security	event,	and	provides	a	central location	for	analysis	of	log	and	metric	data	collated	from	multiple	sources.
input_row.audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	Cluster	Auditing'	pane,	make sure	the	auditing	dashboard	is	enabled
input_row.remediation: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	Cluster	Auditing'	pane,	click	on 'Enable	Cluster	Auditing	Now'	button	to	enable	Cluster	Auditing. Default	Value: By	default,	cluster	auditing	is	disabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug-application-cluster/audit/ 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/91406.htm 269	|	P a g e 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/107745.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 6.2	Activate	audit	logging Ensure	that	local	logging	has	been	enabled	on	all	systems	and	networking	devices. 270	|	P a g e 6.6 Other Cluster Configurations This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	any	remaining	security-related	cluster configurations	in	ACK.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug-application-cluster/audit/ 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/91406.htm 269	|	P a g e 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/107745.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 6.2	Activate	audit	logging Ensure	that	local	logging	has	been	enabled	on	all	systems	and	networking	devices. 270	|	P a g e 6.6 Other Cluster Configurations This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	any	remaining	security-related	cluster configurations	in	ACK.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires checking if cluster auditing is enabled for Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK).
- This is a standard service property related to log management and can be queried using Alibaba Cloud's APIs.
- Alibaba Cloud API and CLI can be used to retrieve cluster configuration details, including whether auditing is enabled.
- The audit involves checking a configuration setting available through the Alibaba Cloud Console, which implies that it can likely be accessed programmatically as well.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ack_cluster_audit_logging_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use Alibaba Cloud CLI or SDK to query the ACK service settings for the specific cluster.
- Specifically, check if the audit logging is configured to send logs to a dedicated datastore such as Alibaba Cloud Log Service (SLS).
- Enumerate all available Kubernetes clusters within the account and region of interest.
- Use the appropriate API/CLI command to retrieve the current configuration of each cluster, focusing on audit logging settings.
- For the Alibaba Cloud CLI, use:
  - Describe the cluster auditing settings by using the appropriate command from the Alibaba Cloud CLI to fetch the cluster's security or logging settings.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - **Pass:** Cluster auditing is enabled and correctly configured to send logs to a remote aggregator.
  - **Fail:** Cluster auditing is not enabled or not sending logs to a remote aggregator.
